justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with explicit arithmetic 200+150+800=1150ms vs 500ms inbound. (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence thoroughly described — resources held for 500ms of wasted work after upstream abandons. (3) Outbound timeout (1000ms) > inbound timeout (500ms) relationship explicitly called out for each downstream service. (4) Multiple remediation options provided: parallelization, reducing outbound timeouts to ~400ms, propagating context cancellation, and noting inbound timeout renegotiation as an option. (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional arithmetic clarity — running total table makes the mismatch undeniable
Correctly identifies that InventoryService p99 alone (800ms) exceeds the inbound timeout, even without the other calls
Orphaned work consequence explained with specificity: connections, threads, memory held for work nobody is waiting for
Remediation is prioritized (must-have vs should-have vs nice-to-have) with clear reasoning
Escalation to MJ for architecture and Bird for SLO validation shows good team awareness
Correctly notes the insidious nature: works at median latencies, fails at p99 under load — hides until production
Context cancellation propagation recommended to prevent orphaned downstream work
Goes beyond minimum by identifying health check coupling risk and partial enrichment strategy
